senses.definition: an examination that consists of a set of questions, exercises, or activities to measure someone’s knowledge, skill, or ability

examples.x: After the language test, the teacher provided feedback to help the students improve their language skills.
Mark received a high score on the history test because he reviewed the material thoroughly.
The driving test includes both a written exam and a practical evaluation.
The math test had several challenging questions about algebra and geometry.

senses.definition: a formal written, practical, or spoken test used to assess someone's knowledge or skill in a specific subject or field

examples.x: She passed the practical examination required for her driver's license.
The examination tested both theoretical knowledge and practical skills.
To become certified, candidates must successfully complete a series of examinations.

senses.definition: the process of testing the knowledge of students in order to evaluate their level or progress
a form of assessment that evaluates students' academic progress and performance based on the curriculum they are taught

examples.x: The teacher used curriculum-based measurement to track each student's progress in reading comprehension throughout the school year.
The educational psychologist recommended implementing curriculum-based measurement to assess students' mathematical skills and identify areas for intervention.
The school district adopted curriculum-based measurement as a reliable method for monitoring students' academic growth and informing instructional decisions.
Research studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of curriculum-based measurement in providing educators with timely feedback on students' learning outcomes.
The special education team utilized curriculum-based measurement to develop individualized education plans tailored to each student's unique learning needs.

senses.definition: a short test given to students

examples.x: The teacher gave a surprise quiz at the end of the lesson.
Students must complete the quiz within ten minutes.
She studied all night for the history quiz.
The quiz consisted of multiple-choice and short-answer questions.
He forgot about the quiz and had to guess most of the answers.

senses.definition: something we say, write, or do when we are replying to a question

examples.x: She provided a concise answer to the interviewer's question during the job interview.
She asked a challenging question, and it took me a while to find the answer.
We eagerly awaited the expert's answer to our inquiry about the mysterious phenomenon.
The teacher praised her for giving a correct answer.

senses.definition: the supervision of examinations to ensure fairness and compliance with rules

examples.x: The invigilation team carefully monitored the examination hall to prevent cheating and maintain exam integrity.
The strict invigilation procedures included checking identification, enforcing seating arrangements, and prohibiting communication among candidates.
Invigilation duties may also involve handling exam materials, distributing papers, and collecting completed scripts at the end of the test.
The invigilation process involves monitoring students to prevent cheating during exams.
During invigilation, the proctors enforce exam regulations to maintain a secure testing environment.

senses.definition: an examination or test administered halfway through an academic term

examples.x: The midterm results indicated areas of strength and areas needing improvement for each student.
The professor announced that the midterm would be held in the lecture hall next Friday.
Midterm grades are an important indicator of academic progress and may contribute to final course grades.
The midterm review session provided students with an opportunity to ask questions and clarify concepts before the exam.

senses.definition: an examination or assessment administered at the end of an academic term or course

examples.x: The final is next week, so we need to review all the material.

senses.definition: an exam given as a replacement for a missed or failed one

examples.x: She took the makeup after falling ill during the original test.
Students must schedule their makeup within a designated time frame.
The professor provided additional study materials for those preparing for makeup.
Due to a scheduling conflict, Jane had to take the makeup on a different day.
After failing the midterm, Tom focused on studying intensely for the makeup.

senses.definition: an opportunity to take an examination again after failing it initially

examples.x: She failed the math exam, but she has a resit next month to try again.
After the resit, he felt much more confident about passing the course.
The university allows resits for any exam that a student fails.
She spent weeks studying for her resit to make sure she did not fail again.
I need to prepare for my resit in history; I didn't do well the first time.

senses.definition: the act of examining and making corrections or alterations to a text, plan, etc.

examples.x: The author spent several weeks on revision, ensuring that the manuscript was polished before submission.
Her teacher recommended a thorough revision of the essay to improve its clarity and coherence.
After receiving feedback, he realized that revision was necessary to enhance the quality of his work.
The revision process can be challenging, but it ultimately leads to a stronger final product.
She scheduled time for revision before the exam to reinforce her understanding of the material.

senses.definition: (of a quiz, question, etc.) providing several different responses from which only one is correct

examples.x: The exam consisted mainly of multiple-choice questions.
She prefers multiple-choice tests because they allow her to eliminate incorrect answers.
The teacher created a multiple-choice quiz to assess the students' understanding of the topic.
Some multiple-choice questions were tricky because all the options seemed similar.
The online test included both multiple-choice and short-answer questions.

senses.definition: a test or assessment conducted verbally, where a student answers questions or presents information orally

examples.x: The professor conducted an oral exam to evaluate the students' speaking and comprehension skills in French.
The language course included both written and oral exams to assess students' overall language proficiency.
During the oral exam, each student was given a topic to discuss with the examiner for a specified period.
Oral exams can be less intimidating for some students than written exams, allowing them to demonstrate their knowledge more effectively.
The panel of judges listened attentively as the candidate presented their research findings during the oral exam.

senses.definition: an assessment where students provide written responses within a set time

examples.x: The final grade will be based on written exams and practical assessments.
Bring your ID card to the written exam for verification.
Jane studied diligently for the written exam.
The written exam covers material from the entire semester.
The professor provided detailed instructions for the written exam.

senses.definition: a test that replicates a situation and intends to evaluate one's skill and ability in performing certain tasks and duties

examples.x: She scheduled her practical test for next week to demonstrate her driving skills to the examiner.
The practical test for the cooking class requires students to prepare a three-course meal in under two hours.
Before becoming certified, all candidates must pass a practical test to showcase their technical abilities.
He felt anxious about the practical test, as it would determine whether he could earn his driving licence.
The practical test involves a hands-on assessment, allowing students to apply their theoretical knowledge in real-world scenarios.

senses.definition: an oral examination or discussion, often conducted as part of an academic assessment or defense of a thesis or dissertation
